description_long: This object is a compact, olive-drab colored bomb with a polygonal, bulbous main body and a flat, angular tail section. The main body appears to be made of metal, with a slightly mottled, matte finish, and is attached to a metallic band and a rectangular mounting bracket. A prominent metallic lever or safety handle is affixed to the side, likely serving as a triggering or arming mechanism. The tail section is reinforced with a metallic strip and features a hexagonal ring, possibly for handling or securing the device. Subtle markings or stenciled text are visible on the side, suggesting military or industrial origin. The overall construction is robust, with visible seams and hardware indicating a functional, utilitarian design.
description: A compact, olive-drab bomb with a polygonal metal body, metallic band, and a prominent safety handle and mounting bracket.
description_view_0: The bomb's main body and metallic safety handle are clearly visible, with the hexagonal ring and mounting bracket in the foreground.
description_view_1: The elongated tail section and metallic reinforcement strip are prominent, with the stenciled markings visible on the side.
description_view_2: The rounded, polygonal top of the bomb is seen, showing the full curvature and the attachment of the tail section.
description_view_3: The side profile highlights the metallic band, safety handle, and the angular transition from the main body to the tail.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The bomb features a prominent metallic safety handle and a mounting bracket, suggesting a mechanical or impact-triggered detonation mechanism.
